items.headline: Equinix Data Center Permitted at Great Oaks Boulevard
items.summary: A $138 million data center expansion receives permit at 131 Great Oaks Bl. Equinix is a global operator of data infrastructure serving cloud and tech companies, and this project represents one of the largest permitted construction projects in the recent batch.

items.headline: Chick-fil-A Remodel and Expansion Permitted on Blossom Hill
items.summary: The existing Chick-fil-A location at 1162 Blossom Hill Rd receives a $810,000 permit for demolition and addition work. The project will reconfigure or expand the fast-food restaurant's footprint.

items.headline: Congregation Kol Emeth permitted to add glass enclosure to trellis
items.summary: The synagogue at 4175 Manuela Avenue received a permit to add glass above a portion of its existing trellis, which will increase the building's lot coverage. The project modifies the congregation's outdoor space.

items.headline: Agreement approved for Seeds of Peace Monument at Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: City Council approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for a Seeds of Peace Monument artwork by Mario Chiodo, to be located at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The artwork memorializes the Seeds of Peace organization's work.

items.headline: Cupertino General Plan Health and Safety Element update scheduled
items.summary: City Council scheduled a discussion on updates to the Health and Safety Element of the Cupertino General Plan covering 2015–2040. The specific changes or proposals were not detailed in the agenda.

items.headline: City Council appoints permanent City Manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager on May 19, 2026.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates water shortage response and master plan
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed drafts of the 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. These plans set how the city manages water supply and responds to drought conditions.

items.headline: Spring cycle community grants and events funding opens
items.summary: The City Council considered 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program applications in the spring funding cycle on May 18. Residents and organizations can apply for funding to support local events and neighborhood projects.
